title: Recombinant a-synuclein pre-formed fibril generation

steps: Reconstitute 1 mg of lyophilized monomeric synuclein with 100 µL cold sterile DPBS On ice (do not pipet, close tube immediately).
Transfer tubes on wet ice to 4 °C cold room and rotate on tube rotator for 00:10:00 .
Centrifuge for 00:10:00 at 15000 x g, °C, at 4 °C .
Transfer supernatant to clean Protein-LoBind tube.
Prepare 5 µL aliquots of serial dilutions (1:10, 1:25, 1:50, 1:100) and measure concentration with Nanodrop (A280 - MW=14,5 kDa; Extinction coefficient ε for human α-syn = 5,960 M -1 cm -1 ).
Dilute down to final concentration of 5 mg/mL , use 100 µL ( 100 µL - 500 µL ) aliquots for reproducible results.
Take 1 µL - 2 µL aliquot of monomer and dilute to 1 ul/ml for electron microscopy, flash-freeze in dry ice – ethanol slurry.
Seal tubes with parafilm if no tube lock.

Place the tube in an orbital thermomixer with a heated lid for 168:00:00 at 37 °C , shaking at 1000 rpm, °C, . NOTE : At the end of the 7 days, the contents of the tube should appear turbid. The thermomixer must have a lid to prevent condensation formation on the tube lids.
Gently flick the tube to resuspend a-syn fibrils. a. Fibrils for quality control steps can be stored at Room temperature Overnight .
For long-term storage (12-18 months), flash-freeze fibrils in liquid nitrogen and store at -80 °C .
a. 5 µL – thioflavin T binding assay.
